items.description: All new endpoints require authentication (JWT RS256/ES256 validated, not HS256)
Authorization checked server-side for every resource operation — IDOR prevention confirmed
Server-side schema validation on all new inputs (Zod/Valibot/Yup/Joi)
Rate limiting configured on all new endpoints — per-user and per-IP
CORS origin allowlist reviewed — no wildcard on authenticated endpoints
Request size limits enforced — no unbounded body parsing
SSRF protection on any server-side HTTP client — block private IPs and metadata endpoints
Webhook signatures verified with HMAC-SHA256 and replay protection
OpenAPI spec updated for all new endpoints
CSRF protections present on all state-mutating browser-accessible endpoints
Error responses reviewed — no stack traces, internal paths, or schema details
Security events logged for all auth decisions — no PII or secrets in logs
JWT expiry enforced — access tokens max 15 minutes, refresh tokens rotated
No raw SQL string concatenation — parameterized queries or ORM used throughout
Mass assignment prevention — explicit field allowlists, not object spread from request body
Sensitive data (PII, credentials) not included in API responses unless required
API versioning strategy in place — old versions have defined deprecation timeline
Backend dependencies scanned — no CRITICAL CVEs unresolved
Secrets scan clean — no hardcoded credentials or API keys
SAST scan passed with no CRITICAL findings
Threat model completed and reviewed for this API surface change
Health/readiness endpoints do not expose sensitive version or config info
GraphQL introspection disabled in production; query depth limit ≤ 10 and complexity budget enforced to prevent resource exhaustion
Financial and state-changing endpoints protected against replay via idempotency keys or HMAC-timestamped signatures with a time-bound window ≤ 5 minutes
No user-controlled input passed to child_process.exec, execSync, spawn with shell:true, or any shell interpreter — allowlisted command templates only
File upload endpoints enforce MIME type server-side (not by extension), max file size, extension allowlist, and malware scanning; files stored outside web root
All sensitive equality checks (tokens, reset codes, HMAC digests, API keys) use crypto.timingSafeEqual — no string ===, indexOf, or includes comparisons
